protein_name: Ubiquitin-like modifier that plays a crucial role in antibacterial autophagy (xenophagy) through the selective binding of CALCOCO2. Recruits all ATG8 family members to infecting bacteria such as S.Typhimurium. May also play a role in aggrephagy, the macroautophagic degradation of ubiquitinated and aggregated proteins. Belongs to the ATG8 family. Most abundant in placenta, lung and ovary. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.
